channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Teachers union sues over Trump administration's deadline to end school diversity programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new federal lawsuit in Maryland is challenging a Trump administration memo giving the nation’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate “race-based” practices of any kind or risk losing their federal money.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cicero Primary Election: Incumbent Dominick defeats Rodriguez, will serve 5th straight term
item.description: Incumbent Cicero Town President Larry Dominick will serve a fifth straight four-year term, defeating Esteban Rodriguez in the primary election Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rebik, WGN 9

item.title: Public records disputes target of Nevada lawmaker's bill creating an ombudsman
item.description: A tug-of-war lies ahead as groups look to influence legislation designed to resolve disputes over access to public records.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump moves to suspend security clearances of lawyers at DC law firm helping Jack Smith
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday ordered the suspension of any security clearances held by lawyers at a prominent Washington law firm who provided legal services to special counsel Jack Smith, the latest move in the Republican's campaign of retribution over the criminal investigat
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOPers press for the release of JFK, Epstein files: Here’s why they’re not out
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna is asking the Justice Department to share an update on when it plans to release documents related to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Southern border state governor takes on cartels and security with signing of executive order
item.description: Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs signed an executive order this week to establish a multi-agency task force to take on cartels and secure the border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Upheaval and firings at CDC raise fears about disease outbreak response
item.description: Staff and observers worry that the agency may not be prepared for emerging threats including bird flu and insect-borne diseases.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Social Security says higher payments are on the way for millions of former public workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 3.2 million Social Security recipients who received pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and other public service jobs will soon see a boost in their benefits.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Soros Static
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadians panic over 'American oppression' after discovering longstanding Google Maps parks designation
item.description: Google is working to update its map system to reflect Canada's "provincial park" designations, removing "state park" designations for the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New bills look to track student absenteeism data faster, hopes to find solutions
item.description: In 2023, more than a quarter of students missed at least 10 percent of school. Some legislators want the state to reconsider how it’s addressing chronic absenteeism.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Germany's conservative election turn points to 'Trump effect internationally,' expert says
item.description: President Donald Trump's successful runs that prioritized halting illegal immigration have had an effect on international elections in places such as Germany and Italy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal watchdog agency intervenes in Trump’s purge of probationary employees
item.description: U.S. Special Counsel Hampton Dellinger has asked the Merit Systems Protection Board to temporarily reinstate six federal employees fired from their jobs and is considering ways to seek relief for others.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Court ruling lets White House ban on the Associated Press continue, for now
item.description: The Trump administration may continue — for now — to keep the AP from covering key events. A federal judge declined to issue a temporary restraining order on Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: VA axes another 1,400 employees amid outcry over transparency
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs on Monday fired another 1,400 employees amid outcry over a lack of transparency from the agency after 1,000 workers were axed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: 5 signs that a U.S.-Europe split is widening
item.description: As the Trump administration ramps up its rhetoric against Ukraine and NATO, European countries are taking note — with increasing alarm.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Smog checks every 2 years good enough under new proposal at Nevada Legislature
item.description: Smog checks would be required once every two years instead of annually under legislation sponsored by a Democrat and a Republican in the Nevada Senate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New FBI Director Kash Patel is sworn in as acting ATF chief, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New FBI Director Kash Patel was sworn in Monday as acting chief of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, taking the helm of two separate and sprawling Justice Department agencies, according to a person familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Northstar commuter trains from Minneapolis to Big Lake could be replaced with bus service
item.description: After more than 15 years, the days of Northstar commuter rail service between Minneapolis and Big Lake may be numbered. MnDOT and the Met Council are considering replacing Northstar commuter trains through the northwest Twin Cities metro area with buses.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court turns down chance to claw back abortion clinic buffer zones
item.description: The Supreme Court turned down an opportunity to overturn its precedent permitting buffer zones around abortion clinics over the objections of two of the court’s leading conservatives. In two orders issued Monday, the court declined to take up challenges to ordinances in Carbondale, Ill.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers
item.description: Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent asking them to document their achievements.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE claims it has saved billions, but nearly 75% unaccounted for
item.description: DOGE hasn't provided evidence for approximately $38 billion in alleged savings — nearly 75% of its total claim.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Young Democratic social media star, frustrated by campaigns, sees another path to turn Texas blue
item.description: Olivia Julianna, 22, loves politics but hates the in-fighting she sees in too many Democratic campaigns in the state.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Katharine Wilson, KPRC 2

item.title: In window before special election, House Republicans push for votes on their agenda
item.description: With a slim majority, Republicans in the Minnesota House are pushing forward bills that are likely not to pass a floor vote. They hope it will still send a political message.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox and Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris receives prestigious Chairman's prize at NAACP Image Awards
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris stepped on the NAACP Image Awards stage with a sobering message, calling the civil rights organization a pillar of the Black community and urging people to stay resilient and hold onto their faith during President Donald Trump’s tenure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump‘s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A U.S. district judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the administration from terminating or changing federal contracts they consider equity-related.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
